name: Coffee Roasted Fruit with Cinnamon Twists
recipeIngredient: Zest And Juice Of 2 Large Oranges
4 Small Apples, Cortland, Peeled, Cored And Left Whole
4 Bananas, Firm And Ripe, cut into 2-inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large, wide nonreactive saucepan, combine the coffee, brown sugar, zest and juice of the oranges, ginger, and cinnamon stick. Bring to a boil.
2. Lower the heat and simmer for 4 minutes.
3. Add the apples and return to the simmer. Gently simmer until the apples are cooked through and tender, testing with a toothpick.
4. Add the bananas for about a minute or so just to heat through. Cooking time will vary depending on the type and size of fruit used.
5. When tender, remove from the heat. Scoop 1 cup of the poaching liquid into a small saucepan.
6. Leave the fruit in their liquid while making the sauce.
7. Dissolve the cornstarch in the orange juice and add to the small saucepan containing the cup of poaching liquid. Bring to a simmer and cook for 3 minutes until lightly thickened.
8. Remove from the heat and set aside to cool.
9. Serve the fruit sliced in shallow bowls or on plates. Spoon some of the sauce over and around the slices; garnish with fresh orange segments and mint sprigs. Serve the Cinnamon Twists on the side.
Yield: 8 servings
